tile.mechanismSummary: Synthetic analog of endogenous GHRH(1-44) that binds pituitary somatotroph GHRH receptors, stimulating endogenous GH release and downstream hepatic IGF-1 production.
tile.localization: anterior pituitary somatotrophs (GHRHR); downstream effects in liver (IGF-1) and adipose tissue
clinicalUses: reduction of excess visceral abdominal fat in HIV-infected adults with lipodystrophy

dosing.quick: FDA-approved: Egrifta SV 2 mg SC once daily in abdomen; Egrifta WR 1.28 mg SC once daily in abdomen (bioequivalent, lower volume).

steps: tesamorelin binds pituitary GHRHR
Gs activation -> adenylate cyclase -> cAMP -> PKA
somatotroph GH synthesis and pulsatile release
hepatic GHR activation -> JAK2/STAT5 -> IGF-1 secretion
IGF-1 and direct GH effects on adipose tissue -> reduction in visceral adipose tissue (VAT)

expanded.humanEvidence: Two pivotal 26-week multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled Phase 3 trials in HIV-infected patients with abdominal lipohypertrophy supported approval. Tesamorelin 2 mg SC daily reduced visceral adipose tissue (VAT) versus placebo. IGF-1 rises to steady state within approximately 2 weeks of daily dosing. Effect on weight is neutral; tesamorelin is explicitly not indicated for weight-loss management.
expanded.animalEvidence: Rat toxicology studies showed hydrocephaly in offspring at systemic exposures approximately 2-4x the human clinical exposure during organogenesis/lactation. Preclinical data support the GH/IGF-1 axis pharmacology.
expanded.mechanismDetail: Tesamorelin is a 44-amino-acid GHRH(1-44) analog N-terminally modified with a trans-3-hexenoyl group that confers resistance to DPP-4 cleavage and prolongs half-life relative to native GHRH. It agonizes the class B GPCR GHRHR on pituitary somatotrophs, elevating intracellular cAMP, activating PKA, and producing pulsatile endogenous GH secretion rather than exogenous GH replacement. GH then acts at hepatic GHR (JAK2/STAT5) to elevate IGF-1 and on adipose GHR to promote lipolysis, selectively reducing visceral rather than subcutaneous fat. Because the pituitary remains the rate-limiter, the physiologic feedback loop is largely preserved, unlike exogenous rhGH.
expanded.safetyDetail: Most commonly reported adverse reactions in HIV patients are arthralgia, injection site reactions, and peripheral edema. Fluid retention (carpal tunnel, arthralgia) and impaired glucose tolerance reflect GH-axis activation. Long-term cardiovascular safety has not been established per FDA labeling. The label includes contraindications in active malignancy, disrupted hypothalamic-pituitary axis (pituitary tumor/surgery, head irradiation), pregnancy, and hypersensitivity. Evidence in non-HIV populations, healthy adults, or older adults (>65) is limited per the label.

safetyRisks.label: Glucose intolerance / worsening diabetes
safetyRisks.helpSummary: Reflects physiologic GH-axis activation; monitor glycemia.
safetyRisks.harmSummary: GH-driven insulin resistance can unmask or worsen type 2 diabetes.

safetyRisks.harmSummary: Consider risk/benefit in patients without meaningful VAT reduction.

safetyRisks.label: Hypersensitivity / injection site reactions
safetyRisks.helpSummary: Local and occasional systemic hypersensitivity reported.
safetyRisks.harmSummary: Serious hypersensitivity reactions have been reported; label warning applies.

enhancementPotential.summary: Off-label and research interest exists for visceral-fat reduction and GH/IGF-1 elevation in non-HIV populations, but FDA approval is HIV-lipodystrophy-specific and the label explicitly notes Tesamorelin is weight-neutral.
enhancementPotential.caveat: Efficacy and safety in non-HIV adults, healthy volunteers for performance/anti-aging use, and pediatric populations are not established. Longer-term cardiovascular and neoplastic risk in such populations is unknown.

safety/efficacy in non-HIV obesity or metabolic disease
pediatric safety (label excludes children with open epiphyses)

clinicalUses: chronic weight management in obesity/overweight (Wegovy SC; Wegovy tablets)
MACE risk reduction in T2D with CVD (Ozempic)
MACE risk reduction in adults with CVD and obesity/overweight (Wegovy)
noncirrhotic MASH with moderate-to-advanced fibrosis (F2-F3) (Wegovy, accelerated approval August 15, 2025)

dosing.quick: FDA-approved: Ozempic T2D 0.25->2 mg SC weekly; Wegovy obesity 0.25->2.4 mg SC weekly; Rybelsus T2D 3->14 mg oral daily; Wegovy tablets for CV/weight indications (approved Dec 2025).
dosing.adminRoute: subcutaneous (Ozempic, Wegovy injection) or oral (Rybelsus, Wegovy tablet)

channelsTransporters: KATP closure in beta-cells (indirect via glucose metabolism/cAMP)

steps: semaglutide binds GLP-1R on pancreatic beta-cells
Gs -> AC -> cAMP -> PKA/EPAC2
glucose-dependent insulin exocytosis; alpha-cell glucagon suppression
vagal afferents and hypothalamic POMC activation reduce appetite
delayed gastric emptying
chronic effects: weight loss, HbA1c reduction, reduced MACE, hepatic fat and inflammation improvements

expanded.humanEvidence: Large randomized Phase 3 programs support each indication: SUSTAIN (T2D, Ozempic), PIONEER (oral T2D, Rybelsus), STEP (obesity, Wegovy), SELECT (17,600+ adults with CVD and overweight/obesity; MACE reduction demonstrated), and ESSENCE (noncirrhotic MASH F2-F3, accelerated approval August 15, 2025). In SELECT, MACE occurred in 6.5% on Wegovy versus 8% on placebo, supporting the 2024 CV indication. Wegovy tablet approval added in December 2025.
expanded.animalEvidence: Rodent carcinogenicity studies in mice and rats showed dose- and duration-dependent thyroid C-cell tumors (adenomas and carcinomas) at clinically relevant plasma exposures. Human relevance remains undetermined; this is the basis for the boxed warning.
expanded.mechanismDetail: Semaglutide is a 31-residue GLP-1 analog engineered for albumin binding via a C18 fatty-diacid side chain on Lys26 (extending half-life to ~1 week) and Aib8 substitution for DPP-4 resistance. GLP-1R activation on pancreatic beta-cells elevates cAMP and enhances glucose-dependent insulin secretion while suppressing alpha-cell glucagon. Central GLP-1R activation in hypothalamic arcuate POMC neurons and the area postrema reduces appetite and caloric intake. Peripheral effects include delayed gastric emptying. Cardiometabolic benefits in MACE and MASH trials appear to involve weight loss, improved insulin sensitivity, reduced hepatic steatosis/inflammation, and possibly direct vascular effects not fully characterized.
expanded.safetyDetail: Boxed warning for rodent thyroid C-cell tumors (MTC); contraindicated in personal/family history of MTC or MEN 2. Warnings: pancreatitis, acute gallbladder disease, hypoglycemia (especially with insulin/SU), acute kidney injury secondary to GI volume depletion, hypersensitivity including anaphylaxis and angioedema, diabetic retinopathy complications (notable in SUSTAIN-6), increased heart rate, and suicidal behavior/thinking. In SELECT, participants >=75 years reported more hip and pelvic fractures on Wegovy than placebo. Most commonly reported adverse events across programs are gastrointestinal and dose-related.

enhancementPotential.summary: Regulatory-approved uses now span diabetes, obesity, MACE reduction in CVD with overweight/obesity, and noncirrhotic MASH with F2-F3 fibrosis. Use outside these indications (e.g., cosmetic weight loss at low BMI, healthy adults, or off-label indications without the required comorbidities) is not FDA-sanctioned.
enhancementPotential.caveat: Efficacy and safety have been established within defined trial populations. Extrapolation to healthy, non-obese, or non-diabetic adults for cosmetic or performance purposes lacks regulatory support; long-term safety in such populations is unknown.

dosing.quick: FDA-approved: 2.5 mg SC weekly starter, titrated monthly by 2.5 mg increments to maintenance 5, 10, or 15 mg weekly; additional 7.5 and 12.5 mg doses available. Sleep apnea indication uses 10 or 15 mg weekly.

steps: tirzepatide engages both GIPR and GLP-1R on beta-cells and other tissues
Gs -> AC -> cAMP -> glucose-dependent insulin secretion, alpha-cell glucagon suppression
central GLP-1R signaling in hypothalamus/area postrema reduces appetite
delayed gastric emptying
GIPR signaling in adipose tissue may enhance lipid handling and insulin sensitivity
net effects: HbA1c reduction, body weight loss, improved AHI in OSA

expanded.humanEvidence: SURPASS program supported the T2D indication (Mounjaro, May 2022). SURMOUNT-1 and SURMOUNT-2 supported the chronic weight management indication (Zepbound, November 2023). SURMOUNT-OSA supported the 2024 sleep apnea indication: in participants using PAP, tirzepatide reduced AHI by ~29 events/hour versus ~6 on placebo; in those not using PAP, ~25 versus ~5 on placebo, with mean weight loss approximately 45-50 lb on tirzepatide versus 4-6 lb on placebo.
expanded.animalEvidence: Rodent carcinogenicity studies showed thyroid C-cell tumors, triggering the class boxed warning shared with other GLP-1-containing incretin agonists.
expanded.mechanismDetail: Tirzepatide is a 39-residue peptide built on a modified GIP backbone with Aib substitutions at positions 2 and 13 for DPP-4 resistance and a C20 fatty-diacid (gamma-Glu-2x OEG) on Lys20 for albumin binding and extended half-life (~5 days). It engages both GIPR and GLP-1R, activating Gs-coupled cAMP/PKA signaling. At beta-cells, glucose-dependent insulin release is enhanced and glucagon is suppressed; in the hypothalamus and area postrema, appetite is reduced; gastric emptying is delayed. GIPR activation may additionally promote adipose insulin sensitivity and lipid handling. In SURMOUNT-OSA, the AHI reduction was accompanied by substantial weight loss, suggesting the OSA benefit is in large part mediated by weight-loss-driven reduction in upper airway fat and loading.
expanded.safetyDetail: Class warnings include boxed warning for rodent thyroid C-cell tumors/MTC; contraindicated in personal/family history of MTC or MEN 2 and in serious hypersensitivity to tirzepatide. Labeled warnings include pancreatitis, gallbladder disease, hypoglycemia with insulin/SU, acute kidney injury (volume depletion), hypersensitivity, diabetic retinopathy complications, and suicidal behavior/ideation monitoring per class. GI adverse events are most common and dose-related; dose escalation mitigates them.

tile.mechanismSummary: Single-molecule triple agonist of GIP, GLP-1, and glucagon receptors; adds glucagon-receptor activation to the GIP/GLP-1 dual-agonist concept, with the intent of increasing energy expenditure and hepatic lipid mobilization on top of appetite suppression and glycemic effects.

steps: retatrutide engages GIPR and GLP-1R on pancreatic and CNS sites (glucose-dependent insulin secretion, appetite suppression, delayed gastric emptying)
engages hepatic GCGR -> increased hepatic glucose output and lipid mobilization; increased resting energy expenditure
net effect in obesity trials: weight loss exceeding dual-agonist benchmarks at the 12 mg dose

expanded.humanEvidence: As of April 2026, retatrutide is not FDA-approved and remains in Phase 3 TRIUMPH development under Eli Lilly. TRIUMPH-4 (NCT05931367) was a 68-week, randomized, placebo-controlled Phase 3 trial in adults with obesity/overweight and knee osteoarthritis (n=445, randomized 1:1:1 to 9 mg, 12 mg, or placebo) and reported topline results December 11, 2025 including approximately 28.7% mean body weight reduction at the 12 mg dose and improvements in WOMAC pain score. A second Phase 3 readout, TRANSCEND-T2D-1, was announced March 19, 2026. Seven additional Phase 3 readouts are expected in 2026 across obesity, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular and renal outcomes, MASLD/MASH, OSA, and chronic low back pain. Phase 2 data (previously reported) showed up to ~24% weight loss at 48 weeks at the 12 mg dose.
expanded.animalEvidence: Preclinical pharmacology and animal data from Lilly support triple-receptor engagement and weight-loss efficacy; detailed published animal data remains sparser than for approved incretin agonists.
expanded.mechanismDetail: Retatrutide is a lipidated peptide engineered for once-weekly SC dosing that agonizes GIP, GLP-1, and glucagon receptors from a single molecule. GLP-1R and GIPR engagement drives appetite suppression, glucose-dependent insulin secretion, and delayed gastric emptying, similar to tirzepatide. The added GCGR agonism at the liver raises hepatic glucose output and lipid mobilization and modestly increases resting energy expenditure, which is hypothesized to account for the larger weight-loss magnitudes observed. The exact receptor-activity ratio and biased signaling profile are proprietary.
expanded.safetyDetail: Publicly reported safety signals include typical incretin-class gastrointestinal adverse events (nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation) and a dose-dependent cutaneous dysesthesia signal (skin sensitivity/tingling/tenderness) reported at the highest TRIUMPH-4 dose (approximately 20.9% of participants on the 12 mg dose in secondary reporting). Because retatrutide is investigational, the full safety profile, long-term cancer signals, and cardiovascular outcomes are not yet established. Any non-trial supply (compounded or 'research peptide') is outside FDA-approved channels and not quality-controlled.

safetyRisks.harmSummary: Non-trial supply is unregulated; identity, purity, and sterility cannot be guaranteed.

enhancementPotential.summary: Phase 2 and initial Phase 3 (TRIUMPH-4) data suggest weight-loss efficacy exceeding currently approved GLP-1 and GLP-1/GIP agonists, with approximately 28.7% mean weight loss at the 12 mg dose in a 68-week Phase 3 trial.
enhancementPotential.caveat: Retatrutide remains investigational. Early-access obesity data comes from populations with obesity/overweight plus comorbidities, not healthy adults. Safety, durability, and rebound effects after discontinuation have not been fully characterized. Non-trial 'research peptide' sourcing is outside regulated manufacturing and is not an approved medical product.

expanded.humanEvidence: LEAD program supported initial T2D approval (Victoza, January 25, 2010). SCALE program supported obesity approval (Saxenda, December 23, 2014). LEADER demonstrated MACE reduction in adults with T2D and established CV disease, supporting the CV risk-reduction indication. Pediatric T2D (Victoza) was approved in 2019 for ages >=10 y. First generic Victoza (liraglutide injection 18 mg/3 mL) was FDA-approved in December 2024; first generic Saxenda was approved and launched in August 2025.
expanded.animalEvidence: Rodent carcinogenicity studies demonstrated dose- and duration-dependent thyroid C-cell tumors at clinically relevant exposures; human relevance remains undetermined. Rat studies showed no effect on fertility at doses up to 0.6 mg/kg.
expanded.mechanismDetail: Liraglutide is a 31-residue GLP-1 analog with ~97% sequence homology to human GLP-1. The peptide precursor is produced in Saccharomyces cerevisiae and then acylated with a C16 palmitic acid via a gamma-glutamic acid spacer on Lys26; position 34 is substituted from Lys to Arg to ensure a single acylation site. The fatty acid enables reversible albumin binding, extending plasma half-life to approximately 13 hours and supporting once-daily dosing. At the GLP-1R, liraglutide activates Gs/cAMP/PKA signaling to enhance glucose-dependent insulin secretion and suppress glucagon. Central GLP-1R activation in the hypothalamus and area postrema reduces appetite. Chronic administration lowers HbA1c, reduces body weight, and reduces MACE in the T2D+CVD population studied in LEADER.
expanded.safetyDetail: Boxed warning for rodent thyroid C-cell tumors/MTC applies; contraindicated in personal/family history of MTC or MEN 2. Labeled warnings include pancreatitis, gallbladder disease, hypoglycemia (especially with insulin/SU), acute kidney injury (volume depletion), hypersensitivity including anaphylaxis and angioedema, and in adult SAXENDA trials a numerical imbalance of breast cancer events among women (17 of 2379 on SAXENDA vs 3 of 1300 on placebo) whose causal interpretation is unclear and is flagged in the label. Anti-liraglutide antibodies develop in approximately 2.8% of adult SAXENDA-treated patients, with neutralizing antibodies in ~1.2%; in pediatric SAXENDA trials, 12% developed anti-liraglutide antibodies and ~1% had cross-reactivity with native GLP-1.

safetyRisks.label: Thyroid C-cell tumors (rodent; boxed warning)
safetyRisks.helpSummary: Rodent MTC signal drives label warning.
safetyRisks.harmSummary: Contraindicated in personal/family history of MTC or MEN 2.

safetyRisks.label: Breast cancer imbalance signal in SAXENDA adult trials
safetyRisks.helpSummary: Numerical imbalance in adjudicated cases; causality unclear.
safetyRisks.harmSummary: 17/2379 on SAXENDA vs 3/1300 on placebo (adult female participants).

safetyRisks.label: Pancreatitis
safetyRisks.helpSummary: Class effect; monitor for severe abdominal pain.
safetyRisks.harmSummary: Labeled warning; discontinue if suspected.

safetyRisks.label: Gallbladder disease
safetyRisks.helpSummary: Class effect amplified by rapid weight loss.
safetyRisks.harmSummary: Cholelithiasis and cholecystitis reported.

safetyRisks.label: Hypoglycemia with insulin/secretagogues
safetyRisks.helpSummary: Higher risk with insulin or sulfonylurea.
safetyRisks.harmSummary: Consider insulin/SU dose reduction on initiation.

safetyRisks.label: Acute kidney injury
safetyRisks.helpSummary: Usually secondary to GI-driven volume depletion.
safetyRisks.harmSummary: Postmarketing AKI including dialysis-requiring cases.

safetyRisks.label: Hypersensitivity (anaphylaxis, angioedema); anti-drug antibodies
safetyRisks.helpSummary: Serious hypersensitivity reported; antibodies may occur.
safetyRisks.harmSummary: Neutralizing antibodies may reduce response or associate with injection-site reactions and low glucose.

enhancementPotential.summary: Approved uses span T2D (including pediatric >=10 y), MACE reduction in T2D with CVD, and chronic weight management (including pediatric >=12 y with body weight >60 kg). Liraglutide has been surpassed in potency and weight-loss magnitude by semaglutide and tirzepatide but is now available as a generic, changing access dynamics.
enhancementPotential.caveat: Efficacy and safety are established in the trial populations studied. Off-label use in populations not meeting BMI or comorbidity criteria is not FDA-sanctioned and lacks the same evidentiary basis. Daily injection burden relative to weekly GLP-1 RAs is a practical limitation.

missingEvidence: long-term oncologic outcomes (MTC, breast cancer signal adjudication)
safety in type 1 diabetes
pregnancy data (avoid use)
head-to-head long-term CV outcomes vs semaglutide
anecdotalUse: off-label use for non-indicated weight loss outside labeled BMI thresholds

claims.value: Acylated GLP-1 analog with 97% homology to native human GLP-1; GLP-1R agonist

Victoza: T2D in adults and pediatric >=10 y, and MACE reduction in T2D with CVD. Saxenda: chronic weight management in adults with obesity/overweight+comorbidity and pediatric >=12 y with body weight >60 kg and obesity.
C172H265N43O51, MW 3751.2 Da; 31 residues; Arg34 substitution; C16 palmitoyl on Lys26 via gamma-Glu spacer
Victoza 0.6 mg SC daily starter titrated to 1.2 or 1.8 mg; Saxenda 0.6 mg SC daily titrated weekly to 3 mg
First generic Victoza approved December 2024; first generic Saxenda approved and launched August 2025 (Teva)
SAXENDA adult trials: adjudicated breast cancer in 17/2379 on liraglutide vs 3/1300 on placebo; causal interpretation unclear
